source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Dropped Object Incident, Eugene Island Block 307A

context.region: Gulf of America, Eugene Island Block 307

actions_taken: BSEE onsite Incident Follow-up Investigation conducted on 19 March 2025
Elevators inspected and found worn and not properly maintained
G-110 (C) Incident of Non-Compliance issued to Guardian on 21 March 2025
event.summary: On 17 March 2025 at 2140 hours, a near miss dropped object incident occurred during well abandonment operations at Eugene Island Block 307A. A single joint of 3.5-inch pipe fell approximately 40 feet to the deck after striking a Self-Retracting Lifeline (SRL) Arm in an elevated hydraulic jack basket. The pipe struck a hydraulic hose and wheel unit fire extinguisher handle. No injuries or pollution occurred. Root causes included inadequate communication between crane operator and floor hand (banksman), blind lift conditions, inadequate procedures, inadequate supervision, and equipment failure due to poor maintenance of elevator latching mechanism.

hazards.name: Crane Operations in Confined Deck Area

threats.name: Crane operator loss of line of sight during pipe lowering
Inadequate communication between crane operator and banksman
Pipe contact with SRL Arm in elevated basket
Elevator latch mechanism failure due to worn spring
Floor hand manual intervention to dislodge pipe

consequences.name: Dropped pipe object striking deck and equipment
Potential personnel injury from falling object

controls.name: Radio Communication System between Crane Operator and Banksman
controls.barrier_role: Communication barrier to ensure coordinated lifting operations

supporting_text: There was no radio communications with the floor hand in the elevated hydraulic jack basket and the crane operator
The procedure for pulling out of the hole with work string was not followed since there was limited hand communication and no radio communication between the floor hand and crane operator

controls.name: Clear Line of Sight for Crane Operator
controls.barrier_role: Visual control to prevent blind lifts and contact with obstacles

supporting_text: He did not have a clear line of sight of the pin end of the pipe as it came to rest on the Self-Retracting Lifeline (SRL) Arm
The crane operator was unaware that he lowered the joint of pipe down onto the SRL Arm
The crane operator was conducting a blind lift and did not have adequate controls in place

controls.name: Hand Signal Protocol and Banksman Identification
controls.barrier_role: Visual communication and identification of designated signal person

supporting_text: The hand signals given by the floor hand in the elevated hydraulic jack basket were inadequate and ignored by crane operator

Inadequate supervision since management failed to follow the Tubing Pulling Procedure which states that the floor hand acting as the banksman was to be identified with a florescent green safety vest or hard hat net

controls.name: Daily Work Plan Hazard Analysis
controls.barrier_role: Identification and control of hazards in confined deck laydown area

supporting_text: BSEE reviewed the Daily Work Plan and found that it did not adequately address the hazards associated with setting down pipe with the crane in a small laydown deck area

Inadequate hazards analysis. The deck area where the 3.5-inch pipe was been laid down was very small and not clearly defined in the hazard analysis. There were no barriers in place to define a safe zone or identify potential hazard locations

controls.name: Elevator Preventive Maintenance Program
controls.barrier_role: Ensure elevator latching mechanism functions properly to retain pipe

supporting_text: BSEE inspected the elevators and observed that they were worn and not properly maintained

The elevators that were being used for the 3.5-inch pipe lift were not properly inspected and maintained. The elevator's latching mechanism malfunctioned and the manufactured spring that holds the elevator's latch closed was worn and very weak

controls.name: Tubing Pulling Procedure Compliance
controls.barrier_role: Standardized procedure to ensure safe pipe handling and communication protocols

supporting_text: management failed to follow the Tubing Pulling Procedure which states that the floor hand acting as the banksman was to be identified with a florescent green safety vest or hard hat net
The procedure for pulling out of the hole with work string was not followed since there was limited hand communication and no radio communication between the floor hand and crane operator

controls.name: Supervision and Compliance Monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Ensure procedures are followed and hazards are controlled during operations

supporting_text: Inadequate supervision. The Supervisor did not ensure that the floor hand acting as the banksman in the elevated hydraulic jack basket was identified with a fluorescent green safety vest or hard had net as stated in the Tubing Pulling Procedures

controls.name: Personnel Awareness and Avoidance
controls.barrier_role: Workers in drop zone able to detect and avoid falling object

supporting_text: There were two workers in the area where the 3.5-inch pipe struck, but they were able to move out of the way before the pipe landed on the deck

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
